reviews.review_description:
Edit: the game is actually really good. There were a few situations I wasn't quite sure of but that was because I didn't realise how interactive the environment was. 

I just wanted to say though that the reason why I didn't buy this game was because I thought it was going to be the same as Memoreum. I liked Memoreum but i just didnt want to play the same game twice. After playing the demo I can see it is quite unique so it has given me motivation to consider buying it.

I don't know if this demo reflects the quality of the finished product but whatever this is wasn't fun and is in need of polish. It was slow, boring, frustrating to play, with janky hand controls. With all the high ratings I expected a much more engaging and better functioning experience. It was quite the opposite.

The visual graphic effects are great and the genre for this story is new and fresh for VR and sorely needed. This is a great demo!!!!  I don't know how you made a demo with the qualities of Half life Alex so I hope you have more games coming our way. I can't wait for the full game! Well done guys

reviews.review_title: Turn off ASW as soon as you begin
reviews.review_description:
Solid game, thanks for the demo. ASW should be off by default on quest 3. The game ran much smoother after I disabled it.

Enemies could be more dangerous. Not difficult.

reviews.review_title: ok I get it more now
reviews.review_description:
So at first I said this was beautiful and boring. I do think it has a very long intro type play but, maybe more games should do that to make sure you really get it. 

I really feel like this game might be amazing. I've only played the demo. 
physics are amazing, One of my favorite things. 

I just really hope it's a long game. It must be because I've been playing the demo forever lol. 

I heard that there aren't any people in it and I have to admit that would be a buzz kill but there are plenty of different really creepy robot spider things That totally will jump up on you and scare the life out of you if you're not expecting it lol I didn't know they did that. 
very reminiscent of Metro.

graphics are absolutely incredible. I think it's one of the best graphic games out there and it plays very very smooth. You have your choice of 72 Hertz 80 or 90 Hz or even 120 but I'm pretty sure that's only with desktop.

The demo is what uve tried so far and its visually Impressive. I've only seen a few enemys so far and it's Def interesting! I like human enemies and havntnseen any so far ,but I'm also in the demo, but the way everything's set up and following the reason for humanity's fall, made me enjoy the robotic vibe of everything. I'm poor af so let's hope the full game coming out today is of a fair price lol
5 stars for me so far...p.s. LUL=]

At first I was so excited by the atmosphere, climbing, and exploration. But it got boring real fast. The story,  dialogue, and voice acting are bland and cliche. "I've lost my d**n memory... Who the h**l am I?" Seriously?

The simple cube-based graphics work for the environment and initially impressed me for such a small dev, but the enemies are just not scary at all. 

Half-Life Alyx works because it's an interesting story with top tier voice acting and genuinely scary enemies. 

Edit: OK, this was a bit harsh. If you've never played HLA, this is the closest we've seen so far on the Quest. It is impressive what they have accomplished and it's not fair for me to hold them to PC VR standards. Plus the dialogue is getting less annoying by the end of the demo, so there's hope. Increasing to 3 stars for now.

I’m a Metroid fan and the dystopian feel of this brings the same general feeling. I did get some glitching in the matrix a couple times (when you’re climbing in the tunnel and see the card on the crate outside the hole, don’t pull it to you or yours may also end up stuck in the rocky formation making you have to start back at the last save).  I’m not the best at unloading/reloading so I wish I could just reload but that’s a me issue.  The concept is pretty interesting so far and I will be watching for the full release. I’ll be interested to see if there are more creatures / mechanical species to contend with in the rest of the game because so far, it’s the same 3ish and it was starting to feel repetitive.

Holy crap this is awesome. I instantly wishlisted the game. Devs keep up the good work. This demo screams “My devs care about making this game”. It clearly wants to be half life alyx for quest and so far it isn’t doing bad at all. Can’t wait to see what the final polished version looks like!

reviews.review_title: Has to be seen to believe!
reviews.review_description:
My first time playing this game, expectations were low. Most demos from relatively unknown developers don't have much in the way of quality. 

But, holy cow! I'm blown away by the sheer quantity and quality of this gameplay in this demo. Castello has really made something special here. 

I can't wait until the full game releases. It just jumped to the top of my list of most anticipated games!

It's clear that the devs are putting their hearts and souls into this game. The "data world" parts are awesome and beautiful - give us more of that, please!

One note to devs: the health boosters don't seem to work for me. Could be a bug. Hit register on the baddies' pink lights also seems to be inconsistent.

In any case, I can't wait to see where this goes!

developer_response.body: Ehmmm... we substituted the holsters with the backpack in the last update, you now need to reach over your should to get stuff. We changed it because people were having difficulties when holding the gun to use the gravity grab as the pockets will get in the way. The backpack solution is a way clearer one.

reviews.review_description:
I've been playtesting Of Lies and Rain since last year, and I was captivated by its potential even in the early stages of development. While it's labeled as a demo, it feels more like a tantalizing glimpse into what’s to come. If you enjoyed the Red Matter series, this game is definitely for you.

Of Lies and Rain is a story-driven first-person shooter designed from the ground up exclusively for VR. Set in a dark, sci-fi, post-apocalyptic world (and no, it's not another zombie game), you’ll battle to reclaim your past and save humanity from the brink of extinction.
 
The world you once knew has vanished, along with your memories. You’re guided by an unfamiliar voice, pushing you to confront the AI responsible for a devastating 20-year war that has forced humanity underground. The surface is now dominated by hostile machines and new lifeforms struggling to survive alongside you.
  
Prepare for a journey through a dystopian narrative where nothing is as it seems, and every encounter tests your judgment of friend versus foe. As you navigate the ravaged environments, take your time to explore. Collect ammo and red cubes known as GPUs, essential for upgrading your weapons and acquiring resources. 

Your left wrist features a watch that tracks your GPU count and health status. The weapon grab mechanic stands out as one of the best I've seen in VR – simply press the B button to choose from your arsenal, and use the Y button to access saved health syringes. 
 
This demo offers a mere taste of the immersive experience awaiting you in the full game. The cliffhanger ending will leave you eager for more, so be sure to wishlist Of Lies and Rain.

I've playtested the entire game and this is something you don't want to miss. For VR gamers seeking an engaging narrative and thrilling gameplay, Of Lies and Rain is a must-try. I promise that you'll be impressed. Check out my Of Lies and Rain Demo showcase video on YT @misschiefvr

reviews.review_title: A fun sci-fi game in a demo package
reviews.review_description:
This was a great demo worth the time. It does a lot to let you know what the game is about (story, mechanics, feel, etc). It has me looking forward to the full game. One of the things that impressed me was how well for VR it was made. Lots of environmental interactions. 

There are minor issues (removing a clip always removed two, frame rate). But many fully published games have many more issues.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic demo has my kind of jam
reviews.review_description: So pleasantly surprised, it has a metro awaking / living remain vibe to it. Two game's I loved. Great simple mechanics with that creepy metro sci-fi feeling. It's just my perfect jam, nice upgrade system easy to understand game play so far. Loving just jumping into a game head on without a long drawn out basic tutorial that seems to never end. This game let's u learn and if u have played similar games u know what to do right away. Nice job developers,  I will buy the full release day one. Love these kind of games. Brillant start .

reviews.review_title: Still needs work, but interesting start
reviews.review_description:
Adding feedback here in the hope the devs take it on board. I realise it’s an unfinished demo, so take with a pinch of salt, and i’ll mostly mention improvements or issues.

- minor issue on QPro with hand alignment being offset. The hands in game were further forward than they should be. Not an issue in any other game (i tested to be sure)
- framerate issues, but i’m sure this is known as it’s just a demo. Plus it was a QPro and not Q3
- a few gameplay elements felt way too much of a HLAlyx rip off than anything original. Which left it all coming off as a cheap  repliacerather than anything interesting or novel. Enemies are a rip off from alyx/half life. Gun selection UI. The sound effects for gravity gloves, the way gravity gloves work etc.
- a lot of janky interactions where weapons such as hammers etc felt jelly-like. As their movement was smoothed. It also didn’t help that they didn’t seem to do any melee damage (unless that was a bug). I expected a knife or hammer to interact with the bodies in for instance.

As the title says this game is heavily influenced by HL:A and that's no bad thing. I first played the PCVR Steam demo and really enjoyed it and then followed it up by playing the Quest 3 build to see how it held up by comparison and it was very accomplished. Obviously missing dynamic lightning etc. but certainly no slouch against the obviously more powerful PC counterpart. The demo is around an hour, so you really get a good taste of this game and I will almost certainly be picking this up (hopefully it's cross buy). There's still some polishing to be done i.e. boxes don't break when you throw them at the floor like Alyx and the melee weapons feel a bit floaty in the hand (crow bar), but it's on the right path. Looking forward to the final release based on this demo. Well done Devs.

reviews.review_title: Can’t wait to see the finished product!
reviews.review_description:
This demo blew me away. This game is going to be an absolute win for VR. As long as the developers fix a some of the jank. Although this demo does plays better than most finished games out there. This is my most anticipated release for this year in vr now.  

Developers,
 please make sure you release this game as polished as possible. What you have here is way too good to be released undercooked.  You guys are doing a Great job and I can’t wait to see what your studio bring us in the future!

developer_response.body:
Hey!!
Thanks a lot for your kind words about our game! We're taking our time on this and we want to polish it as much as possible. we're about to update the demo with some fixes on the problems youplayers have pointed out! In the meanwhile we're working on the final game! Finger crossed!!! :)

reviews.review_description:
The captivating story in the appealing apocalypse setting, combined with the familiar controls, makes the gameplay very enjoyable.
The official release of this game is highly anticipated.

Absolutely blown away by this demo. Every single inch of this game feels perfectly polished, and after just an hour of playing it, I'm hooked on the story, wanting to know how everything got the way it was and what I need to do. The graphics and visuals are incredible, and the environments are some of the most unique of any vr game I've ever played. My only small complaint (which isn't really even a complaint) is that the weapons feel more like tools for solving puzzles than weapons. This works fine in the game, but it's more like shooting stationary aliens to get rid of lasers in your way and shooting little spider things that slowly move towards you, instead of intense gunfights and frantic action. Now again, as it is this works fine in the game, and this is also just the demo so it's possible the full game already has more intense combat with humanoid enemies that really fight back. What made the combat feel so good in half life alyx is the realistic behavior of the guns, and the combat feedback. They were still sci-fi guns, but they shot real bullets, and felt like they actually carried serious power behind them, and hitting enemies was met with satisfying feedback and visuals that made it feel extremely rewarding. But again, not even a huge deal, and the game is incredible so far

developer_response.body: You'll definitely have some better fights in the rest of the game!! but please do not expect AAA quality, we're a small indie studio and don't have the budget nor the experince to make something of that quality! btw we're glad you enjoyed the game full release is not too far away!

reviews.review_description: I put this on my wishlist as soon as it showed up on coming soon. It's been a while and I hate that there isnt a release date. I happened to be glancing at developers other games on some of my coming soon games and I saw this had a DEMO. I was excited and downloaded it immediately. I really liked it and can't wait for the full version. Its my kinda game! Please hurry!!!!

Really enjoyed it overall and will definitely buy it on release.  I just love the physics systems and all the little ways the random items can be used to interact with the world. From using the metal as a shield, to smashing glass and reaching through to unlock the door. So many games have just the weapon as the sole way to interact with the world and its a lost opportunity. Definitely eager to see if there's any more novel uses of the physics systems in the rest of the game. There's a small amount of physics related jank and performance issues when loading, but nothing too bad.  The matrix world is amazing - fun movement mechanics are another thing I absolutely love to see in games.  The initial enemies though are perhaps a weakness. So far they are a bit lackluster, but this is ok in the first level as long as it picks up later on. I think the force pull on the gloves could be tuned better. Many times I had to try repeatedly to pick up ammo from the ground only for it to not go high enough to grab. Finally the lighting, loved the art direction of contrast with the bright reds over the greys, but its very uniformly bright at times even in areas that don't look like they are directly lit. Hopefully this feedback helps a bit.  I look forward to seeing the whole game.
Playing through the demo was great. Had a very Half life Alyx fell to it. Graphics were amazing for a stand alone game and the mechanics were solid. Looking forward to the full release.

Late to the party (as always), I have only just picked up and played through this insanely impressive first level demo and am now highly motivated to pick up the full release when it drops, game runs ridiculously smooth on my quest 3, I had only one strange and humourous glitch throughout my playthrough (tried to pick up a padlock, that I'd shot off a locker and the game decided I must die lol), the obvious comparisons to the Half-Life games are there to behold, but the game also displays its own identity, the locations are glorious for standalone vr and my only quibble would be with the default sound levels, but these are changeable within the settings... Excellent game so far and fingers crossed the full release continues to impress.

reviews.review_title: Is gut. Wait for full release.
reviews.review_description: It's a good demo, decent hour to kill for free. Gotta wait for the full release where it's at though!
This is the best demo I ever played on Quest.

developer_response.body: Best review we've seen on Quest!!!

reviews.review_description: I was really getting into this, but then that digital world stuff. Kicked my a**, I died 5 or 6 times I still haven't mastered it yet. I don't understand what it's all about anyway. Maybe they could put an easy setting to make that part easier. Other than that, I was really having a good time.
This game is awesome, everything I've ever wanted in a VR game! I NEED THE FULL VERSION NOW!!!!!
At a glance it's trying to be Alyx, but there's so much more to it. Needs polish graphically, but functionally it's very impressive.

Hi, after playing this Demo, the game is definitely good, and it's like a mix of Half Life Alyx & Boneworks, and the Nintendo switch game called Crisis, I realise this is just a very good Demo, but a few things I noticed that could be more polished;

* when you eject your Gun clip and add a new one, 2 fall from your backpack instead of one, so grabbing 1 would be good.
* Also will a backpack be added to the game that you can pull out and store items? 
* shooting at Targets feels a little off sometimes as i tried to shoot an enemy, and I used 8 bullets, and it still didn't die.
* the Hand Menu option is good, but can be a bit awkward when pressed, as it pauses your game, will the glove touch pad be more detailed later, as it'd be better if it was on the right hand and the left hand to be used for more interactions, i.e, checking Health, Map, etc?
* will their be a running option?
* when drawing on the chalkboard in the bunker classroom, erasing with the eraser, is a bit choppy as you have to just pat the board to clear it, instead of rubbing It out.
* will the items that you can pick up will they be more usable, I.e. lighter, pills, bottles, etc?
 These are In no way criticisms, it's just what i have observed,  but all in all I am really looking forward to the Full release, Well Done so far guys! 😊

Instant purchase the moment this game drops! I’ll be glued to my Oculus 3, energy drink in hand, diving into endless hours of gameplay. The graphics are absolutely spectacular, with vibrant visuals that pull you right into the action. The sound design and music are immersive and complement the experience.  This game  keeps you hooked from start to finish!

Loved this demo so far but only thing I see that is a must add for us gamers that hav less accuracy then a stormtrooper, is difficulty levels 😬🔫Great story and  very unique but can say it reminded me of genotype and thats one of my fav games I own. Very thankful everytime i found ammo when I did caus down to one bullet only left the entire intro so couldn’t play level 2 if u don’t have that in settings😬😟 But way to go devs awesome demo, other devs should take notes on mor demos to b longer then 5 mins so big ty for that!!
I will be waiting for this drop… the demo was fantastic! Thanks Devs… will support you guys!

I found this game on Reddit, and I honestly didn't think it looked good; however, after playing this demo, I have completely changed my mind. The one thing that makes me really like the game is the finger physics. I genuinely love that. Thank you and have a good (whatever time it is where you are). 😘

This demo is girthy, and well worth your time.  I'm excited for the final release.

It wears its influences on its sleeve.  Particularly Half Life Alyx and System Shock (which is a good thing).  Perhaps some others I'm not familiar with.  But it's different enough to be its own thing.

It's not perfect, and I experienced some performance drops on my Quest 2, in some areas, but most of the time performance and graphics are both very good.

I also found one typo on a sign that said "requiered" instead of "required"

developer_response.body:
Hi FragmentalStew!

Thanks a lot for plying and thank for this review! We'll fix the typo!

reviews.review_title: High potential let down by graphics
reviews.review_description:
This ambitious Half Life Alyx inspired sci-fi adventure could end up becoming a real hit on the Quest, but it still needs a lot of work.

WARNING: SPOILER AHEAD!

(I don't know how to give proper feedback to the developers without a spoiler.)








Note that I tested this on the Quest 3.

Up until the point when I got sucked into the digital realm I was pretty disappointed.  The digital realm hints at greatness. It felt surreal, immersive, and unnerving being there, it was my favorite part of the demo.

Outside the digital realm the graphics are very immersion breaking.

While the resolution is high, the environments and objects are generally very flatly lit.  Interactive objects are evenly lit and overly bright to the point where they don't feel like they're in the environment at all, they look pasted on. There's almost no dynamic lighting and when there is, it's also even and flat looking.  The high resolution combined with the lack of atmospheric lighting makes the game feel like a graphics demo rather than an immersive world.

The atmosphere desperately needs more contrast, shadows, and dynamic lighting.

I would much rather see a game like this run in low resolution with atmospheric lighting and effects than have it run in high resolution and look like this.  Games like Metro Awakening show what is possible, even on the Quest 2.

Unfortunately many gamers judge graphics quality based solely on resolution. Art direction and atmosphere are way down their list of priorities. They're a very vocal group so if the graphics get an update in atmosphere at the expense of resolution there will be outrage, so maybe a Quest 3 update with an "atmosphere or resolution" setting would keep everyone happy.

As others have mentioned some of the VR interactions are a bit clunky, but most work well. The force grab is especially nice as the visual effect that goes with it looks very cool.

Unfortunately the combat was a bit frustrating. The first enemies I met were little bullet sponges so before I could get used to efficiently reloading I'd burnt through all my clips and died.

To sum it up, I really want this game to succeed but without a major update to the graphics I'm not sure if I'll purchase it.

developer_response.body:
Hi Jioxx!!!

Thanks a lot for playing our game and taking the time to leave feedback!
We agree with your feedback, but to be honest, we did everything we could graphically. We agree Metro looks better, but you're talking about one of the biggest and most successful studios in the VR space with millions in budget and likely dozens of people working on it. We're a team of five with basically no funding, and this is only our second game ever, ARK&ADE was our first. As you can imagine, good graphics are very expensive to produce.

We’d love to reach a point where our studio can create an experience as graphically pleasing as Metro, but we’re just not there yet. Sry about that.

We understand if the graphics are a dealbreaker for you, but hey, we did our best!

Thanks again for playing our game and providing feedback. We’re glad you liked the dataworlds, they’ll be bigger and better in the final version. Those in the demo are really just tutorials, and we’re already working on fixing the bugs present. An update should be up soon.

reviews.review_title: While it is a demo, it’s a great one
reviews.review_description: Look, this is just a demo of the game so it isn’t meant to be as amazing as the real game but as a demo, it is awesome. Yes, it definitely needs some work and yes the death screen is boring but this is only a fraction of what is to come. This game has the mix of styles like half life, mixed with metro awakening, also mixed with a little bit of stride fates in a way. This will be an amazing game when it comes out but for now, I’d just ask for the devs to take as long as they need to make this one of the best VR games out there (At least on quest).

developer_response.body: We are taking our time to make this game the best we can. We're a small team of 5 people, so please do not expect anything close to metro or Alyx. But we're doing our best!

reviews.review_description:
Clearly it takes a lot of inspiration from Half-Life Alex, though lacks as much polish and is a little bit more..... "cumbersome" feeling compared to HLA.  Also everything kind of feels a little too slow for some reason, it's hard to put my finger on it.  Feedback isn't as significant when interaction with the world perhaps.  Climbing felt a bit off but functional, maybe it's the lack of feedback/haptics.  Gunplay also felt so far a bit meh, again I think it's really a haptics issue if maybe a dash of graphical feedback being lackluster on enemies.

Graphically it's quite impressive though not the most impressive I've seen, and the demo does have a few hiccups here and there.

Clearly they went for the physics-based approach to most of the world, this could be good or bad depending on your perspective.  It's well enough done, though as I said it's not quite as refined as HLA.  You will probably see that comparison a lot, they borrow a lot of mechanics and ideas.

Don't get me wrong I'll definitely give it a go when it's released.  The story definitely could have some potential, and I'm curious enough to see it through.

As far as this demo, it shows significant promise. Especially if it's continuing to be polished up prior to release.  VR releases tend to get way over hyped so take reviews so far all with a grain of salt IMO.

First off thanks so much to the Devs for giving us such a great demo 

& I've been playing vr for 6 years & tonight was the first time I felt my legs go as I ascended the interior of the train at the start ... Seriously !! 😳 

I cannot wait for the full release .. looks great even in early access & not came across any major bugs as of yet .. got an apex construct on steroids kinda feel ... Defo gonna love this one 

1000% take my cash !!!! 

Cheers 🍻 

Carl

It's a good start. But the low budget shows.
Not in the graphics, which are pretty decent, the interactions which are superb, or even the game design, which is original with a style that stands out from typical productions.
But in the staging and the way the story is told.
That's mainly where you can tell it doesn’t have the budget of Alyx or even Metro Awakening.


I tried the Steam version, but it doesn’t work with Virtual Desktop, and in SteamVR I have a controller bug. I’ll try again later. I hope the textures and lighting effects will make a difference, because right now it feels a bit like an old game, with ugly textures and no shadows on the Quest 3.

If this demo is a just a taste of what the final game will be, it will easily one of the best games in the store.

As a suggestion to the devs:please add stepping sounds and the ability to run. 

Looking very much forward to it!!

Im very impressed with this game. It looks stunning and has so much attention to detail. This is a contender for game of they year already. Heavy Alyx and metro vibes. Superb graphics and effects, great physics, top soundeffects and awesome gameplay.

reviews.review_title: Amazing demo, straight to my wishlist.
reviews.review_description: This demo is absolutely fantastic, it immediately reminded me of playing half-life alyx. I like the upgrade system and the quality for quest 3 is great, 100% buying this on release.

What a great demo!

Highly agree with all the other fantastic reviews.

Will be waiting for the full release indeed!
I just completed the demo, and I love it! I'm looking forward to the full release.

I'm loving it so far and can't wait for full release. 
Loving the artwork.
